releases.title: Independent learner • portfolio builder • at-home barista

details.heading: What's new
items: Completed The Modern Javascript Bootcamp, covering fundamentals, async programming, API integration, Node.js.
Built v1 of personal website from scratch using vanilla HTML/CSS/JS.
Created a personal weather dashboard using weather.gov and publically available web APIs.
Built various browser-based applications and games to demonstrate knowledge of Javascript & full stack web development while completing The Odin Project.
Added smart home, home media server and OTA DVR projects.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Expanded creative problem solving skills with hands-on project-based learning.
Increased career clarity, seeking roles that combine creativity, technical depth and interesting challenges.
Added smart home automation to optimize morning coffee routine and provide daily caffeine to household members.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Recovery and reflection have reduced burnout that had accumulated from v3.0.0.
Fixed frustration with not being able to build things directly by adding Javascript skills, enabling independent creation.

details.heading: What's new
items: Launched new collaborative products from concept to public beta.
Overhauled internal tools to support SaaS transition.
Added Figma, Jira, and Confluence integrations to the organization's product suite.
Built user feedback loops and conducted interviews that reduced new user time-to-value.
Shipped dozens of improvements for the organization's desktop and web apps supporting global UX design teams.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Made industry transition from insurance software / collision repair industry to UX design tools space.
Upgraded influence-without-authority capabilities with user data.
Increased role flexibility beyond traditional PM duties.
Added remote work capabilities.

details.heading: What's new
items: Expanded value stream ownership capabilities.
Launched an AI-powered automated estimating tool for a key insurance carrier client.
Awarded 2018 Annual MVP Award for impactful contributions to Auto Physical Damage Solutions division.
Achieved subject matter expert status and became go-to resource for estimating knowledge across the organization.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Can now translate complex insurance carrier requirements and statements of work into detailed project specifications.
Cross-team coordination is now smoother and includes support for external partners.
Achieved domain expertise and technical competency. No longer a 'master of none'.
Added pourovers to coffee repertoire. Denounced K-Cups.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Fear of appearing uninformed eliminated with possession of intimate product knowledge.
Influence without authority is now functional.
Patched imposter syndrome.

details.heading: What's new
items: Added experience serving as PM for 3 Agile teams
You can now launch a Voice-of-the-Customer(VoC) program from scratch by gathering enough customer insights and sharing product analytics data with stakeholders.
User Acceptance Testing (UAT) suite added to prevent post-launch issues.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Strategic planning and roadmapping have improved with better understanding of implementation details.
Influence increased within the organization, enabling dependency coordination and cross-team prioritization.
Mentorship skills can be activated to share expert knowledge and train new PMs.
Faster customer feedback response times with mastery of in-app product feedback tools.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Resolved inability to say 'no' to requests that don't align with the product direction.
Fixed uncertain prioritization framework.

details.heading: Known issues
items: Cross-team coordination remains challenging.
Pushback from some departments over unclear expectations around unstructured VoC program.

details.heading: What's new
items: After being promoted, Frankie can now be be deployed and embedded in an agile development team.
New Coordination suite added for SAFe Agile Framework integration. Includes sprint planning, retrospectives, daily standups and backlog grooming.
Added usery story authoring capabilities.
UX collaboration module activated for user discovery, ideation and interactive prototyping.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Product decision-making granted. Can now set team priorities and influence direction.
Increased developer credibility through consistent question-asking and coursework.
Built new relationships with key stakeholders, customers and technical teams.
Coffee quality standards increased significantly with exposure to French press and fresh, locally-roasted beans.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Reduced anxiety related to imposter syndrome through supportive team environment that encouraged 'learning-by-doing'
Fixed tendency to nod along when confused. Frankie now asks questions when the meaning of something is unknown.

details.heading: Known issues
items: Difficulty saying 'no' to decline customer requests still persists.
Priorization framework incomplete. Trade-off evaluation still requires additional resources.
Project specifications lack prototyping capabilities.
SAFe practices are not yet fully ingrained.

details.heading: What's new
items: Added experience in the collision repair and auto insurance software industry.
Custom reporting and data visualization in Tableau capabilities are now available.
New Travel Package can be enabled to deliver insightful presentations to key accounts across the United States.
Added cross-functional integration with call centers, third-party vendors and internal teams for Voice-of-the-Customer feedback loops.
Established rapport with product, engineering, and sales team leaders.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Data Analysis and Troubleshooting skills are now capable of resolving client data issues to ensure reporting accuracy.
Added Client Coaching to Presentation Mode to help clients make sense of satisfaction metrics and take meaningful action.
Deepened understanding of what drives customer satisfaction.
Identified need to influence product direction, not just help clients navigate product limitations.
Interest in Product Management added as a logical career path, combining business, technology, user understanding, and design.
Coffee sophistication slightly improved to include break room espresso machine with questionable coffee syrup bags.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Troubleshooting is now more stable when investigating and resolving client data issues.
Resolved nervousness when advising collision shop owners and industry veterans with way more experience.

details.heading: Known issues
items: Frustration level increases when valuable customer feedback is surfaced, but can't directly influence what actually gets prioritized.
Ad hoc reporting dashboards for individual clients lacks automation and does not scale well.
careerPath remains undefined. Internal testing is ongoing and has recently revealed a potential path forward. Stay tuned!
Technology troubleshooting remains unstable when dealing with family members that rhyme with 'rad'.

details.heading: What's new
items: Added professional marketing experience.
Integrated digital marketing and web analytics capabilities.

Following a successful beta, the Spanish language pack is now officially live and available for all Personal & Professional accounts in the Latin America region. Note: known issues processing Castellano (Spain) Spanish still exist.

details.heading: Improvements
items: Enhanced data analysis skills are now capable of building KPI frameworks and tracking website performance
Upgraded Presentation Mode to enable data-driven reports and summarize key insights for management
Confidence level has been increased when operating in Ambiguous Startup Mode

details.heading: Known issues
items: Business operations knowledge remains limited.
careerPath remains undefined.

details.heading: What's new
items: New Bachelor of Arts degree in International Business from San Diego State University. Summa cum laude (not lorem ipsum). Includes emphases in Marketing, Spanish & Latin America.
Added international experience at Universidad del Desarrollo, Santiago, Chile.
Laundry self-sufficiency unlocked

details.heading: Improvements
items: Career clarity improved after identifying desire to help others, leverage technology, understand human behavior and solve complex problems creatively,
Team collaboration skills added
Self-awareness now recognizes that college is a tool, not a silver bullet for life preparation.
You'll notice increased independence and confidence levels when operating outside of the Comfort Zone, even when locale is set to non-US regions.
The Spanish language pack (beta) upgrade now includes fast-paced Chilean Spanish. Pronouncing the final "s" sound is now optional, ¿Cachai?
Nutritional diversity expanded to include vegetables and sushi. Eggs remain unsupported in most formats.

details.heading: Bug fixes
items: Resolved fear of surviving independently in foreign environments.
Fixed a problem where Procrastination Mode would randomly activate in team projects, causing delays. Note: some uninspiring solo projects are still vulnerable to delays.

details.heading: Core features
items: All Star Personality (as voted by peers in the yearbook)
Strong performance in academic environments
Proficient in absorbing life lessons through history teachers, MTV, Harry Potter and conscious underground hip hop lyrics

details.heading: Known issues
items: Console error appears when asking for future plans: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'careerPath')".
Belief System directory is polluted with false narratives.
Nutritional diversity limited to mom's spaghetti and unusual amounts of Parmesan Goldfish crackers.
Unable to import caffeine without sugar integration enabled. Coffee routine remains undiscoverable.
Laundry capabilities do not yet exist.
Procrastination mode automatically activates when a project has 'uninspiring' property set to 'true'.
